name: RETROPERITONEAL LIPOSARCOMA
definition: slow-growing tumor that displaces rather than infiltrates surrounding tissue and rarely

demographics.Frequency: most common (33%) of primary malignant retroperitoneal sarcomas; 95% of all

dominant_finding: mixed pattern: focal fatty areas (-40 to -20 HU) + areas of higher density (+20 HU)
distribution: mixed pattern: focal fatty areas (-40 to -20 HU) + areas of higher density (+20 HU)
discriminating_features: solid pattern: inhomogeneous poorly marginated infiltrating mass with contrast

CT: solid pattern: inhomogeneous poorly marginated infiltrating mass with contrast
mixed pattern: focal fatty areas (-40 to -20 HU) + areas of higher density (+20 HU)
pseudocystic pattern: water-density mass ← averaging of fatty + solid connective-tissue
calcifications in up to 12% (= important sign of dedifferentiation of an initially well-
MR: intermediate SI on T2WI; more hyperintense for myxoid type ← mucopolysaccharide
loss of fat signal intensity on fat-suppressed sequence
ANGIO: hypovascular without vessel dilatation / capillary staining / laking

clinical.Prognosis: local recurrence in 90%; most radiosensitive of soft-tissue sarcomas; 32% overall
